title: Grading the Graders: How ESPN's Trade Grades Hold Up Against Reality
methodology: ESPN grades at time of trade vs. CATV scores computed with 3-5 years of actual player production (win shares, playoff WS, championships, accolades). Only trades from 2021 and earlier included — minimum 4 seasons of hindsight.
sources: ABC News (mirrored ESPN Insider articles unpaywalled, 2014-2019)
RealGM forums (Gasol to TOR, Harris to PHI)

trades.description: Tobias Harris to DET for Brandon Jennings, Ersan Ilyasova
trades.analysis: Pelton got the winner right. Harris gave Detroit years of production. The grade inflation (A for what turned out to be B-level value) is typical but the directional call was correct.

trades.description: MCW to CHI for Tony Snell

MIL.key_assets: Tony Snell (9.6) — reliable 3&D starter for multiple seasons

CHI.key_assets: MCW (0.1) — out of the rotation within a year

trades.analysis: Pelton gave Chicago a B for acquiring MCW — who produced 0.1 CATV and was out of the rotation quickly. Tony Snell, graded C-, became a reliable starter and produced 9.6 CATV for Milwaukee.

trades.description: Kyle Korver to CLE for Mo Williams, Mike Dunleavy, 2019 1st (top-10 protected)
trades.analysis: Correct winner. But Pelton's A for 8.0 CATV (C+ value) shows the chronic grade inflation — giving top marks for modest production.

trades.description: Jusuf Nurkic + MEM 1st to POR for Mason Plumlee + 2018 2nd
trades.analysis: One of Pelton's best calls. Got the winner right AND the margin was reasonable. Nurkic became Portland's franchise center. Plumlee was a fine starter. Both sides got value — Portland just got more. Pelton even underrated Denver (C- for B- value), but the directional call was clean.

trades.description: Serge Ibaka to TOR for Terrence Ross + lower of LAC/TOR 2017 1st

TOR.key_assets: Ibaka (21.3) — 4+ years as a key contributor

trades.analysis: Correct winner. Ibaka gave Toronto years of production across multiple playoff runs. Pelton's best instinct: trusting the established player over the younger asset when the established player actually committed long-term.

trades.description: DeMarcus Cousins + Casspi to NOP for Buddy Hield, Tyreke Evans, Langston Galloway, 2017 1st

SAC.key_assets: Buddy Hield (15.6) — high-volume 3PT shooter for years

NOP.key_assets: Cousins (6.9) — Achilles tear yr 2, left in FA

trades.analysis: Pelton gave Sacramento a D for Buddy Hield — a player he described as having 'poor shooting efficiency.' Hield produced 15.6 CATV. Cousins tore his Achilles and left in free agency (6.9 CATV). The star-for-role-player trade flipped because Pelton didn't price injury risk.

trades.description: No. 1 pick (Markelle Fultz) to PHI for No. 3 pick (Jayson Tatum) + 2018 LAL 1st

BOS.key_assets: Jayson Tatum (95.6) — MVP candidate, franchise player, 5x All-Star

PHI.key_assets: Markelle Fultz (0.5) — forgot how to shoot, traded within 2 years

trades.analysis: The single largest CATV gap in the entire dataset. Pelton called this a dead-even trade (B/B). Boston got Jayson Tatum — a franchise player, MVP candidate, 95.6 CATV. Philadelphia got Markelle Fultz — who forgot how to shoot and produced 0.5 CATV before being traded. The gap between Pelton's grade and reality: 95.1 CATV. No other miss comes close.

trades.description: D'Angelo Russell + Mozgov to BKN for Brook Lopez + No. 27 pick (Kyle Kuzma)

LAL.key_assets: Kyle Kuzma (13.2) — 3+ seasons as rotation player, Lopez (3.8)

BKN.key_assets: D'Angelo Russell (6.2) — traded within 2 seasons, Mozgov (0.4)

trades.analysis: Pelton loved Brooklyn's asset hoarding strategy — 4 separate A or A- grades for Nets acquisitions in 2017-2018. This one didn't work. Russell was traded to Golden State within two years. The No. 27 pick (Kuzma) outproduced the headliner. Pelton graded the name, not the fit.

trades.description: Dwight Howard + No. 31 pick to CHA for Belinelli, Miles Plumlee, No. 41 pick
trades.analysis: Correct winner but both sides produced little. Pelton's A- for 6.8 CATV is classic grade inflation. Howard gave Charlotte one season.

trades.description: Jimmy Butler to MIN for Zach LaVine, Kris Dunn, #7 pick (Lauri Markkanen)
trades.analysis: Pelton gave Chicago the worst possible grade for a package that produced 46.8 CATV including a 2x All-Star (LaVine) and an All-NBA player (Markkanen). Butler gave Minnesota 2 seasons, 1 playoff appearance, then forced his way out.

trades.description: Gallinari to LAC (3-team: LAC, ATL, DEN)
trades.analysis: Pelton gave Atlanta the best grade (B) for a package that produced 0.0 CATV. Denver (B-) also produced 0.0. The Clippers (C-) got Gallinari at 9.3 — the only team that got anything. The grades were backwards.

trades.description: Avery Bradley to DET for Marcus Morris

BOS.key_assets: Morris (9.8) — solid rotation player for 2+ seasons

DET.key_assets: Bradley (-0.2) — left in FA after 1 year

trades.analysis: Correct winner. Pelton's D for Detroit was prescient — Bradley was a rental who left in free agency and produced negative CATV. Morris gave Boston years of solid production.

trades.description: DeMarre Carroll + 2018 1st + 2018 2nd to BKN for Justin Hamilton
trades.analysis: Correct winner. Brooklyn's salary-dump acquisition strategy paid off here — they took on Carroll's contract and got picks. But the A grade for 9.6 CATV (C+ value) is 5 grades inflated.

trades.description: Kyrie Irving to BOS for Isaiah Thomas, Jae Crowder, Ante Zizic, #8 pick (Collin Sexton)
trades.analysis: Pelton gave Cleveland an A for a package built on the assumption the Brooklyn pick would land top-3. It landed at 8. Isaiah Thomas was broken — 32 games for Cleveland. Boston got Kyrie for 2 productive seasons and a conference finals run.

trades.description: Eric Bledsoe to MIL for Greg Monroe + protected 1st + protected 2nd

MIL.key_assets: Bledsoe (20.9) — 3 seasons as Giannis's backcourt partner, championship contributor

trades.analysis: Correct winner and reasonable grades. Bledsoe gave Milwaukee 3 productive seasons including a championship contribution. One of Pelton's best calls — he identified the right side and was conservative rather than inflated.

trades.description: Blake Griffin to DET for Tobias Harris, Avery Bradley, Boban, 2018 1st (Miles Bridges), 2019 2nd

DET.key_assets: Griffin (11.3) — 2.5 productive seasons, All-Star in Detroit

LAC.key_assets: Harris (7.6) — flipped to PHI within a year, Bradley (-0.3)

trades.analysis: Pelton saw the Griffin contract as an albatross (D). Griffin gave Detroit 2.5 seasons and an All-Star nod (11.3 CATV). The Clippers' return scattered — Harris was flipped, Bradley left in FA.

trades.description: Isaiah Thomas + Channing Frye + 2018 1st (Moritz Wagner) to LAL for Jordan Clarkson + Larry Nance Jr.

CLE.key_assets: Nance Jr. (14.8) — 4+ years of production, Clarkson (3.3)

trades.analysis: Pelton gave the Lakers an A for Isaiah Thomas — who played 17 games before being waived. The 2018 first became Moritz Wagner (0.2 CATV). Cleveland got Nance Jr. (14.8 over 4+ years) and Clarkson (3.3). Complete A-to-F inversion.

trades.description: George Hill + Jason Smith to MIL (3-team: MIL, CLE, WAS)
trades.analysis: Correct winner. Hill gave Milwaukee real production and contributed to a championship. But CLE and WAS both got B/B- grades for packages that produced 0.0 CATV. Pelton graded upside that never materialized.

trades.description: Jimmy Butler to PHI for Robert Covington, Dario Šarić, Jerryd Bayless, 2nd-round pick

PHI.key_assets: Butler (9.2) — one playoff run, left for Miami

trades.analysis: Pelton's closest miss. Wrong winner but the margin was 1.75 — nearly a draw. Neither side got lasting value. Most defensible wrong call in the dataset.

trades.description: Tobias Harris + Mike Scott + Boban to PHI for Landry Shamet, Wilson Chandler, Mike Muscala, 2020 1st, 2021 1st

PHI.key_assets: Harris (40.0) — max extension, 5+ years of production, Scott (4.7)

trades.analysis: Pelton gave LAC an A for a package that produced C- value (4.0 CATV). Philadelphia's 'overpay' for Harris (C- grade) returned A- value (44.6). Harris's max contract was mocked — he outproduced the entire return by 10x. The lesson: a player who stays and produces beats a collection of assets that scatter.

trades.description: Marc Gasol to TOR for Jonas Valančiūnas, Delon Wright, CJ Miles, 2024 2nd

MEM.key_assets: Valančiūnas (16.8) — became Memphis's starting center for years, Wright (2.0)

trades.analysis: Pelton gave Toronto the edge (B+ vs C). Gasol contributed to a championship (9.6 CATV) — but Valančiūnas gave Memphis years of starting-caliber production (16.8). Like the Kawhi trade: the championship argument is real, but CATV measures cumulative production, and the team that kept the younger player longer won on volume.

trades.description: Anthony Davis to LAL for Brandon Ingram, Lonzo Ball, Josh Hart, #4 pick, future 1sts

LAL.key_assets: Anthony Davis (64.8) — championship, All-NBA, franchise cornerstone

trades.analysis: Pelton gave New Orleans the edge (A- vs C+) and the CATV says LAL won by 60.4. Anthony Davis won a championship in year one and produced 64.8 CATV. New Orleans got real value back (43.5) — Ingram became an All-Star — but the Lakers won. The C+ for a player who won a championship and became a franchise player is Pelton's structural bias against big-name acquisitions, inverted: he was skeptical of the price, but the player was worth it.

trades.description: Russell Westbrook to HOU for Chris Paul + picks

OKC.key_assets: CP3 (11.6) — 1 elite season, flipped to PHX

HOU.key_assets: Westbrook (4.9) — fit never worked, traded after 1 year

trades.description: Kawhi Leonard + Danny Green to TOR for DeMar DeRozan, Jakob Poeltl, 2019 1st
trades.analysis: CATV says SAS won (volume) but Toronto won a championship. The most philosophically interesting trade in the dataset.

trades.description: Paul George to OKC for Victor Oladipo, Domantas Sabonis

IND.key_assets: Sabonis (34.2), Oladipo (13.9) — Oladipo became MIP yr 1

OKC.key_assets: PG13 (25.0) — 2 great seasons, left in FA

trades.description: Kristaps Porzingis + THJ + Burke + Lee to DAL for Dennis Smith Jr., DeAndre Jordan, Wes Matthews, 2021 1st, 2023 1st
Chris Paul to PHX for Ricky Rubio, Kelly Oubre Jr., Ty Jerome, Jalen Lecque, 2022 1st

PHX.key_assets: CP3 (34.2) — NBA Finals yr 1, 64-win season yr 2

trades.analysis: Pelton's worst miss by magnitude after the Tatum-Fultz swap. OKC's entire A-graded return produced 1.8 CATV. CP3 took Phoenix from lottery to NBA Finals. Pelton was skeptical of a 35-year-old on a big contract. CP3 produced 34.2.

trades.description: Jrue Holiday to MIL (4-team: MIL, NOP, DEN, OKC)

MIL.key_assets: Jrue Holiday (25.5) — championship + 4 seasons of two-way excellence

trades.description: Russell Westbrook to WAS for John Wall + 2023 1st
James Harden to BKN (4-team deal: BKN, HOU, CLE, IND)

CLE.key_assets: Jarrett Allen (53.1!) — All-Star center, Cleveland cornerstone

BKN.key_assets: Harden (13.7) — 1.5 productive seasons then demanded trade

trades.analysis: Pelton gave BKN a D — skeptical of what they gave up. Right instinct: Harden's 13.7 was B- value, not superstar returns. But the real story was Cleveland getting Jarrett Allen (53.1 CATV) as a throw-in nobody discussed.

trades.description: Aaron Gordon + Gary Clark to DEN for Gary Harris, R.J. Hampton, 2025 1st
Russell Westbrook to LAL for Kuzma, KCP, Harrell, #22 pick (Isaiah Jackson) + more (5-team deal)

LAL.key_assets: Westbrook (2.4) — disastrous fit, traded after 1 year

trades.analysis: Pelton's cleanest call. He saw the Westbrook-to-Lakers disaster coming. Westbrook produced 2.4 CATV and was traded after one season.

scorecard.grade_inflation_note: Pelton's median A-graded team produced C+ value (8.5 CATV). His As are worth about 2 full grades less than advertised.
worst_misses: Celtics-76ers pick swap (2017): called it B/B, reality was A (95.6)/F (0.5). Tatum vs Fultz — 95.1 CATV gap.
IT/Clarkson swap (2018): gave LAL an A for 0.5 CATV and CLE a D for 18.1 CATV. Complete inversion.
CP3 to PHX (2020): gave OKC an A for 1.8 CATV and PHX a B- for 34.9 CATV. OKC's entire return was a rounding error.
Harris to PHI (2019): gave LAC an A for 4.0 CATV and PHI a C- for 44.6 CATV. Harris on the max produced 10x the return.
Butler to MIN (2017): gave CHI an F for 46.8 CATV. The only F he ever gave, and it was wrong by 8 grades.
best_calls: Westbrook to LAL (2021): D grade, D reality. Nailed it.
Nurkic-Plumlee (2017): B/C-, reality B/B-. Winner and relative margin both right.
Bledsoe to MIL (2017): B-/C, reality B/D. Championship contributor. Right winner, right direction.
Ibaka to TOR (2017): A-/B, reality B/C+. Right winner, Ibaka gave years of production.
Bradley-Morris (2017): B/D, reality C+/F. Right winner, Bradley was indeed a bad acquisition.
systematic_biases: Grade inflation: Pelton's A grades correspond to C+ actual value on average. His As are overrated by ~5 grades.
Young player discount: F for LaVine+Markkanen (age 22), D for Buddy Hield, D for Nance+Clarkson. Young players in packages get structurally underrated.
Rental trap: Overvalues the team getting the star in rental situations (Butler, CP3, Cousins, Kyrie). Stars leave.
Reputation grading: IT got an A for the Lakers despite being physically broken. Name recognition drives the grade more than projected production.
Afterthought blindness: The most valuable players in multi-team trades are the ones nobody discusses (Allen in Harden trade, Nance in IT trade, Kuzma in Russell-Lopez trade).
